Investigative Summary
---------------------
Huntress has detected persistence artifacts on host 'WINDCHILLVIZ'. These were installed on the host before Huntress was deployed.

Please see the remediation section for next steps.

The Huntress SOC identified the Malware Family as TrickBot.

Remediations:
-------------
Assisted Remediations provided by the Huntress SOC to remediate the incident. These can be executed automatically in the Huntress Platform:
- Delete Service - name: qzngptsdfy
- Delete Registry Key - key: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\qzngptsdfy

All remediations provided can be found in the Huntress Platform: Incident Report: https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429248/infection_reports/1436864#remediations-tab

all_recent.body:
*** The Huntress Agent has already remediated this incident. ***

Host: WS-AI-31 - https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429248/agents/11556938
Organization: ANI - Andersen Industries, Inc.
Tags: None
Security Products: CrowdStrike Falcon Sensor, Windows Defender

Incident Report: https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429248/infection_reports/1428836
Severity: Low

Investigative Summary
---------------------
Huntress detected the following Malware Artifacts on this host:
- TrickBot : This banking trojan is capable of automatically propagating throughout a network and often used to install other malware.

Considering the low risk posed, you can follow the below remediation guidance at your convenience.

* Note: If you continue to receive new malware artifact reports for this host, it is an indication there may be an active infection on the network. See here for more details: https://support.huntress.io/hc/en-us/articles/4404012696467-Remediating-Emotet-Trickbot

The Huntress SOC identified the Malware Family as TrickBot.

Remediation Instructions
------------------------
To remediate, run the following commands from an administrative command prompt (cmd.exe) and perform the actions below:
- sc.exe stop "tnnorwzgui"
- sc.exe delete "tnnorwzgui"
- sc.exe stop "mtwxceuiyu"
- sc.exe delete "mtwxceuiyu"
- sc.exe stop "msgwvxdimq"
- sc.exe delete "msgwvxdimq"
- sc.exe stop "igjtzkpaeb"
- sc.exe delete "igjtzkpaeb"

- Remove the "c:\mswvc.exe" file and ensure it is not recreated.

Footholds
---------
Foothold 1 - https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429248/autoruns/35673876
Service Display Name: TechnoServices3
Service Name: msgwvxdimq
Command: %SystemDrive%\mswvc.exe
File Path: c:\mswvc.exe

Foothold 2 - https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429248/autoruns/35673877
Service Display Name: ServiceTechno4
Service Name: mtwxceuiyu
Command: %SystemDrive%\mswvc.exe
File Path: c:\mswvc.exe

Foothold 3 - https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429248/autoruns/35673878
Service Display Name: Technics-service3
Service Name: tnnorwzgui
Command: %SystemDrive%\mswvc.exe
File Path: c:\mswvc.exe

Foothold 4 - https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429248/autoruns/35673875
Service Display Name: Service_Techno4
Service Name: igjtzkpaeb
Command: %SystemDrive%\mswvc.exe
File Path: c:\mswvc.exe

-------------------------
Thanks again for trusting Huntress and please don't hesitate to reach out to incidents@huntress.io if you have any questions.
